care.tips: Native to: PER, GUY OO, BZE BA, BZE PB, BZL RJ

description: Geniostoma rupestre is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganaceae. It is a shrub or tree which ranges from Taiwan and Malesia to Papuasia, Queensland, New Zealand, and the western and central Pacific islands.
origin: QLD QU, PIT OO, NUE OO, BIS OO, SCZ OO
AUSTRALIA, AUSTRALASIA, NSW NS, NSW, QLD CS
synonyms: ["Logania falcata", "Logania longifolia var. brevifolia", "Logania alpina", "Logania angustifolia", "Logania floribunda", "Euosma albiflora", "Logania revoluta", "Logania floribunda var. brevifolia"]

description: Spigelia anthelmia, the West Indian pinkroot, wormbush, or wormgrass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae. It is native to Mexico, Central America, the Caribbean, Florida, and northern South America through to Bolivia and Brazil, and it has been widely introduced to other tropical locales, including western and west-central Africa, India, Sri Lanka, Indonesia, Thailand, Peninsular Malaysia, Hainan, and the Bismarck Archipelago.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Spigelia marilandica, the woodland pinkroot or Indian pink is a herbacious perennial wildflower in the Loganiaceae family native to inland areas of the Southeastern and Midwestern United States.
origin: East central and southeastern United States

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)

description: Strychnos spinosa, the Natal orange, also called Mokotra in Madagascar, is a tree indigenous to tropical and subtropical Africa. It produces sweet-sour yellow fruits, containing numerous hard brown seeds. Greenish-white flowers grow in dense heads at the ends of branches. The fruits tend to appear only after good rains. It is related to the deadly Strychnos nux-vomica, which contains strychnine. The fruit are large, smooth, and firm, transitioning from green to yellow as they ripen.
